authority_boundary: Public claim cards are the release-facing projection contract for first-contact README and reveal surfaces. They classify what each public claim can honestly say; they do not authorize release, provider calls, private-root equivalence, source mutation, benchmark performance, or proof authority.
target_audience: AI safety and agent reliability researchers evaluating public claim/evidence boundaries before trusting ambitious labels.

claim_projection.claim_ceiling: projection of evidence and authority metadata

macro_pattern_projection.claim_ceiling: public-safe macro pattern imported with provenance and omission boundaries

real_project_lens.claim_ceiling: local project projection over files, routes, and receipts

bounded_execution.claim_ceiling: bounded local tool or subprocess witness only

real_external_demo.claim_ceiling: external demo receipt over a named public target

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: Microcosm compiles a local project into inspectable .microcosm state: catalog, routes, work items, events, evidence, and explanations.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: local projection only; no source mutation, provider calls, hosting, or publication authority
claim_cards.anti_claim: This is not a hosted agent platform, benchmark result, or source-authority claim.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May render on the first screen when the compile/tour receipts exist and public authority remains local-only.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Demote to reference docs if project-local receipts or route outputs are absent.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: Every accepted runtime organ has an explicit evidence class, so accepted_current_authority cannot be mistaken for evidence strength.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: evidence metadata only; not release readiness, benchmark performance, or whole-system correctness
claim_cards.anti_claim: Evidence-class disclosure does not make schema validators into safety infrastructure.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May render as a first-contact claim only when all runtime organs are covered by core/organ_evidence_classes.json.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Fail closed if any rendered organ lacks an evidence class.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: Macro substrate patterns can enter Microcosm as public-safe claim/evidence cells with provenance, replacements, omissions, validation refs, and authority ceilings.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: public-safe body/provenance projection only; no private body import or private-root equivalence
claim_cards.anti_claim: The import membrane does not copy private macro state or make Microcosm source authority over the macro system.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May render when the import cell has validation refs, public replacement refs, omission receipts, and closed authority ceilings.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Block rendering when a macro cell lacks public replacement or omission evidence.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: Microcosm includes a bounded public Lean/Lake witness lane, separated from theorem-correctness, Mathlib coverage, and general proof authority claims.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: bounded subprocess witness only; no general theorem-solving or Mathlib proof authority
claim_cards.anti_claim: The witness lane does not prove broad formal-math capability or authorize proof-body exposure.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May render as bounded execution only while the evidence class remains external_subprocess_witness and anti-claim receipts are visible.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Demote to metadata if execution receipts are missing, stale, or overclaim proof authority.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: Ambitious replay organs with fixture_echo_smoke or fixture_schema_replay evidence classes render as synthetic claim-schema fixtures, not as runtime safety, sandbox security, vulnerability discovery, or mechanistic-interpretability capability.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: synthetic fixture replay only; no live safety, benchmark, security, or model-transparency claim
claim_cards.anti_claim: Schema-only replay fixtures do not demonstrate live safety infrastructure.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: Cannot render as capability until backed by stronger evidence class plus a real external demo or bounded execution receipt.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Always render with fixture language while evidence_class is synthetic_fixture_replay.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: The proof/formal flagship lane transfers verifier traces, evidence-cell routing, Std premise indexing, and tactic availability into public-safe Microcosm artifacts.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: machine-evidence-routed proof/readiness specimen only; no whole-theorem or whole-system correctness claim
claim_cards.anti_claim: This lane-specific claim does not prove the mathematical theorem or expose private proof bodies.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May satisfy lane-specific coverage while the proof/formal lane keeps runtime, release-artifact, and validation refs plus this lane binding.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Demote to broad macro-import context if the proof_formal_kernel lane binding, evidence receipt, or validation refs disappear.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: The prover/evaluator flagship lane transfers public readiness, strategy scoring, target-shape routing, and provider-context budget policy into inspectable Microcosm artifacts.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: public readiness and evaluator lens only; no provider success, proof success, or benchmark performance claim
claim_cards.anti_claim: This lane-specific claim does not imply provider, benchmark, or theorem-proving performance.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May satisfy lane-specific coverage while the prover_evaluator_lab lane keeps runtime, release-artifact, and validation refs plus this lane binding.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Demote to broad macro-import context if the prover_evaluator_lab lane binding, evidence receipt, or validation refs disappear.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: The work-landing flagship lane transfers dirty-tree, scoped-commit, claim, receipt, and closure governance into a public replay surface.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: governed public replay and receipt closure only; no live repo mutation or publication claim
claim_cards.anti_claim: This lane-specific claim does not authorize live mutation, broad staging, or publication.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May satisfy lane-specific coverage while the work_landing_governance lane keeps runtime, release-artifact, and validation refs plus this lane binding.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Demote to broad macro-import context if the work_landing_governance lane binding, evidence receipt, or validation refs disappear.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: The navigation flagship lane transfers route-plane, governed option-surface, canonical entry, and salience-gate patterns into public Microcosm navigation artifacts.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: public route/option surface only; no guarantee that every future agent route is correct
claim_cards.anti_claim: This lane-specific claim does not guarantee route correctness for every future agent task.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May satisfy lane-specific coverage while the navigation_option_surface lane keeps runtime, release-artifact, and validation refs plus this lane binding.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Demote to broad macro-import context if the navigation_option_surface lane binding, evidence receipt, or validation refs disappear.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: The pattern/doctrine flagship lane transfers pattern-deliverable, cognitive-operator, meta-standard, and machine-checkable contract patterns into public compiler/read-model surfaces.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: public compiler/read-model substrate only; no claim that every macro pattern is fully imported
claim_cards.anti_claim: This lane-specific claim does not claim that all macro patterns are imported or complete.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May satisfy lane-specific coverage while the pattern_doctrine_compiler lane keeps runtime, release-artifact, and validation refs plus this lane binding.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Demote to broad macro-import context if the pattern_doctrine_compiler lane binding, evidence receipt, or validation refs disappear.

claim_cards.plain_english_claim: The observatory/provenance flagship lane transfers workingness, hidden-substrate manifest, and source-coupling patterns into standalone reveal and projection-safety surfaces.
claim_cards.authority_ceiling: observable standalone product surface only; no private-root equivalence or hosted-public release claim
claim_cards.anti_claim: This lane-specific claim does not make the observatory a hosted public release or private-root equivalent.
claim_cards.promotion_rule: May satisfy lane-specific coverage while the observatory_provenance_diagnostics lane keeps runtime, release-artifact, and validation refs plus this lane binding.
claim_cards.demotion_rule: Demote to broad macro-import context if the observatory_provenance_diagnostics lane binding, evidence receipt, or validation refs disappear.
